title: Indian Startups See Revival in Late-Stage Funding
author: Startup Hub
content: Bengaluru: After a prolonged funding winter, multiple late-stage Indian startups have successfully raised significant growth capital over the past month, signaling a potential revival in investor sentiment.
Several unicorns in the fintech, e-commerce, and SaaS sectors have announced fresh funding rounds, predominantly led by existing investors along with participation from sovereign wealth funds.

Investors are exhibiting a strong preference for companies showcasing clear paths to profitability, strong corporate governance, and sustainable unit economics. 'The days of growth at any cost are over. Capital is available, but it is reserved for resilient business models,' said a prominent venture capitalist.
This resurgence is crucial as many growth-stage companies were facing runway constraints. The renewed capital infusion is expected to spur hiring and expansion plans in the latter half of the year.
